party_affiliation.name: Party affiliation (IN)
party_affiliation.question_text: What is your preferred political party?

bjp_therm_1.name: BJP thermometer
bjp_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the Bharatiya Janata Party?

inc_therm_1.name: INC thermometer
inc_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the Indian National Congress?

hindu_therm_1.name: Hindu thermometer
hindu_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling towards Hindus in India?

muslim_therm_1.name: Muslim Feeling Thermometer
muslim_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling towards Muslims in India?

general_trust.name: General Trust
general_trust.question_text: Do you think your wallet (or your valuables) would be returned to you if it were found by a stranger?

institutional_corruption.name: Institutional Corruption
institutional_corruption.question_text: If a member of Parliament were offered a bribe to influence the awarding of a government contract, do you think that the member of Congress would accept or refuse the bribe?

options.3: Equally likely to refuse or accept

institutional_response.name: Institutional Response
institutional_response.question_text: If you were to complain about the poor quality of a public service, how likely or unlikely is it that the problem would be easily resolved?

vote_importance.name: Importance of Voting
vote_importance.question_text: How important or unimportant is it to vote in every election?

pride.name: Pride
pride.question_text: How proud are you to be Indian?

fair_treatment.name: Fair Treatment from government authorities
fair_treatment.question_text: Do you agree or disagree that you can expect fair treatment from government authorities?

democracy_importance.name: Importance of Democracy
democracy_importance.question_text: How important or unimportant is it to you that you live in a representative democracy?

norm_judges.name: Norm judges
norm_judges.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: {inparty: party of the respondent} elected officials should sometimes consider using defamation laws and cases to expel {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie}  political opponents and prevent them from standing for elections when those opponents are critical of {inparty}  politicians.

norm_judges_perception.name: Norm judges perception
norm_judges_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: {inparty: party of the respondent} elected officials should sometimes consider using defamation laws and cases to expel {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie}  political opponents and prevent them from standing for elections when those opponents are critical of {inparty}.

norm_polling.name: Norm polling
norm_polling.question_text: Do you agree or disagree:  {inparty: party of the respondent} politicians  should engage in vote buying ahead of elections in areas that typically support {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie}.

norm_polling_perception.name: Norm polling perception
norm_polling_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} politicians should should engage in vote buying ahead of elections in areas that typically support areas that typically support {inparty: party of the respondent}.

norm_censorship.name: Norm censorship
norm_censorship.question_text: Do you agree or disagree with the following: The government should be able to censor media sources that spend more time attacking {inparty: party of the respondent} than {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie}.

norm_censorship_perception.name: Norm censorship perception
norm_censorship_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: The government should be able to censor media sources that spend more time attacking {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} than {inparty: party of the respondent}.

norm_loyalty.name: Norm loyalty
norm_loyalty.question_text: Do you agree or disagree with the following: When a {inparty: party of the respondent} candidate questions the outcome of an election,  other politicians from {inparty: party of the respondent} should be more loyal to the {inparty: party of the respondent} party than to election rules and the constitution.

norm_loyalty_perception.name: Norm loyalty perception
norm_loyalty_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: When a {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} candidate questions the outcome of an election, other politicians from {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} should be more loyal to the {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} party than to election rules and the constitution.

violence1.name: Violence 1
violence1.question_text: Please consider the following scenarios: {name} was arrested for blocking traffic while protesting. He was arrested by police after leading a protest against {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} outside the parliament in New Delhi. He made no effort to leave when asked by police. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence2.name: Violence 2
violence2.question_text: {name} was arrested for property damage. He was arrested by police after he vandalized and damaged several large signs expressing support for candidates of the {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} party. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence3.name: Violence 3
violence3.question_text: {name} was arrested for assault. He was arrested by police for throwing rocks at peaceful {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} protesters. Although no one was seriously injured, one person was taken to the hospital with a head injury. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence4.name: Violence 4
violence4.question_text: {name} was arrested for setting a building on fire. He was arrested by police as he attempted to run from a fire he started at the local {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} headquarters. Although he waited for the building to close for the night, several adjacent buildings were still occupied. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence5.name: Violence 5
violence5.question_text: {name} was arrested for assault with a deadly weapon.He was arrested by police after he attacked a  crowd of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} protesters with a metal rod. Although no one was killed, several individuals were seriously injured and one spent a month in the hospital. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence6.name: Violence 6
violence6.question_text: {name} was arrested for murder. He was arrested by police after surveillance footage was found showing him stabbing a prominent {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} member to death. {name} targeted the victim because he believed the victim had prevented him from voting in the last election as part of a conspiracy to stop {inparty: party of the respondent} voters. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

education_india.name: Education
education_india.question_text: What is the highest level of education you have completed?

options.2: Literate but no formal schooling/School up to 4 years
options.5: Diploma or college certificate but not a graduate
options.6: Graduate or Post Graduate General (B.A., M. A., B Com, BSC etc.)
options.7: Graduate or Post Graduate Professional (MBA, MD, PhD etc.)

lang_speak_in_1.question_text: Which of the following languages do you speak, in addition to English? Please select all that apply

caste_in.name: Income
caste_in.question_text: Gross family income is the combined income of all individuals who receive any income, including wages, salaries or rents, before tax deductions. What is your gross monthly family income?

gross_household_in.name: Household Income
gross_household_in.question_text: What is your monthly gross household income?

religion_in.name: Religious affiliation
religion_in.question_text: Do you regard yourself as belonging to any particular religion, and if so, to which of these do you belong?

geo_state_in.name: India States
geo_state_in.question_text: In which state do you currently reside?

Ideo10.name: Ideology (IN) -- Left|Right
Ideo10.question_text: In political matters, people talk of "the left" and "the right". How would you place your views on this scale, generally speaking?

empl_stat_MENA.name: Employment status
empl_stat_MENA.question_text: Which, if any, of the following options best describes your current employment status?

options.6: Taking care of home or family

lastvoteindia.name: Vote 2019 Lok Sabha elections
lastvoteindia.question_text: At the 2019 Lok Sabha elections, which party did you vote for, or perhaps you didn't vote?
